title: A machine learning-based model for the quantification of mental conflict
data_sources: Rat slot machine behavioral task (Samejima et al. 2009, J. Neuroscience)

data_scope.task: two-armed slot machine with intermittently switching reward probabilities
data_scope.n_subjects: not stated in Research Briefing; refer to companion full paper 10.1038/s43588-023-00439-w
data_scope.outputs: time-resolved curiosity estimate with 68% confidence interval

core_conclusion: Mental conflict — traditionally considered purely subjective — can be quantified as a temporal signal from behavioral data alone. By extending the Free Energy Principle with a curiosity meta-parameter and decoding it via machine learning from rat slot-machine choices, the authors show animals exhibit negative average curiosity (prefer certain rewards) but adaptively spike curiosity after environmental changes, demonstrating active inference with autonomously controlled exploration.
why_it_matters: First quantitative window into reward-curiosity conflict without neuroimaging; bridges RL and FEP into a single parameterized model; opens behavioral biomarker pathway for ADHD, autism, and social withdrawal; decoder operates from observer perspective — no invasive recording needed.
why_nature: Novel algorithmic framework (inverse FEP decoder) with broad transferability; tight proof-of-concept matching Research Briefing format; strong interdisciplinary reach across ML, neuroscience, and clinical psychiatry; endorsed by FEP originator Karl Friston.
added_value_to_my_research: Multi-timescale conflict decoder -> multi-scale accessibility decoder for nested travel-demand conflicts in 2SFCA
Tiny RNN strategy decoder -> interpretable state decoder for patient GPS access-seeking behavior
Distributional curiosity -> distributional R2SFCA with equity measured on access-level tails
SHAP-based conflict explainability -> unified temporal-spatial explainability workflow for health ML
Spatial reward-curiosity conflict -> decoding telehealth adoption willingness from appointment logs

related.title: Multi-timescale reinforcement learning in the brain
related.relation: extends the single meta-parameter to hierarchical timescales of reward-curiosity conflict

related.title: Discovering cognitive strategies with tiny recurrent neural networks
related.relation: replaces scalar meta-parameter with interpretable RNN-decoded cognitive strategies

related.title: An opponent striatal circuit for distributional reinforcement learning
related.relation: extends mean-curiosity estimate to distributional reward expectations in neural circuits

related.title: Stop explaining black box machine learning models for high stakes decisions and use interpretable models instead
related.relation: motivates explainability requirements for the ML conflict decoder in clinical psychiatry

related.title: Reward magnitude determines reinforcement learning efficiency
related.relation: complements the reward axis of the reward-curiosity conflict model with reward-magnitude effects

figures.caption: Decoding reward-curiosity conflict from rat behavioral data. (a) Slot machine task illustration: reward probabilities switch intermittently. (b) Decoded rat curiosity over time with 68% CI — mostly negative (preference for certain reward) with adaptive spikes after probability switches.
